target.name: AlixLabs
target.founder: Jonas Sundqvist, Dmitry Suyatin, Amin Karimi

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
AlixLabs is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by Jonas Sundqvist, Dmitry Suyatin, and Amin Karimi, having raised €15M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M). Public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) exist but cannot be applied given AlixLabs' undisclosed revenue.

The peer-median method is the sole applicable triangulation technique. The four private comparables yield a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. This spread reflects genuine heterogeneity within Series A frontier chip companies—a function of differentiated technical IP, customer traction, and capital efficiency. Public-comp anchoring, while available via a 20.6x price-to-sales median across three semiconductor tickers, cannot be deployed without target revenue disclosure.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on the peer median of $1.5B. Key uncertainties include AlixLabs' revenue run-rate or customer pipeline relative to the peer set, the degree of technical differentiation in its chip architecture, and the timing and sizing of downstream funding rounds. The relatively wide dispersion in peer valuations ($500M–$5.0B) indicates that within-stage positioning varies materially and warrants deeper diligence on product-market fit metrics.
